zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Welles於1569年4月24日出生在英國英格蘭艾可波炳沃斯時，她的父親Robert Welles28歲，母親Alice Hunt26歲。她於1590年9月28日與Sylvester Baldwin在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾爾斯波利結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1630年12月2日在英國史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Welles于1569年4月24日出生在英国英格兰艾可波炳沃斯时，她的父亲Robert Welles28岁，母亲Alice Hunt26岁。她于1590年9月28日与Sylvester Baldwin在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾爾斯波利结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1630年12月2日在英国史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔享年61岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔。

nameMeanings.name: Welles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Wells
meanings: English: habitational name from Wells next the Sea (Norfolk) or Wells (Somerset), both named with the plural of Old English wella ‘spring, stream’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a group of springs or streams.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dupuis   ‘from the well’.
History: One of numerous early immigrants from England bearing this name was Thomas Welles, governor of colonial CT, who was in Hartford, CT, by 1636.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Welles was born on 24 April 1569, in Bobbingworth, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Welles, was 28 and her mother, Alice Hunt, was 26. She married Sylvester Baldwin on 28 September 1590, in Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 2 December 1630, in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Welles est née le 24 avril 1569, à Bobbingworth, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Welles, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Alice Hunt, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sylvester Baldwin le 28 septembre 1590, à Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 décembre 1630, à Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Welles am 24. April 1569 in Bobbingworth, Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Welles 28 und ihre Mutter Alice Hunt 26. Sie hat am 28. September 1590 in Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Sylvester Baldwin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 2. Dezember 1630 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Oswestry, Shropshire, England und wurde in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Welles è nata il il 24 aprile 1569 nel Bobbingworth, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert Welles, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Alice Hunt, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sylvester Baldwin il 28 settembre 1590, nel Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 2 dicembre 1630, nel Oswestry, Shropshire, England, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolta a Oswestry, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Wellesが1569年4月24日にイギリスイギリスエセックスボビングウオルスで生まれた時，父Robert Wellesは28歳，母Alice Huntは26歳であった。1590年9月28日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアチオレスブリーでSylvester Baldwinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1630年12月2日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアオスウエストリーで61歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアオスウエストリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Welles(이)가 1569년 4월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 보브빙워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Welles(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Hunt(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1590년 9월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 컬스버이에서 Sylvester Baldwin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1630년 12월 2일 영국 슈럽실르 오스베스트리에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 오스w스트리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Welles nasceu em 24 de abril de 1569, em Bobbingworth, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Welles, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Alice Hunt, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sylvester Baldwin em 28 de setembro de 1590, em Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 2 de dezembro de 1630, em Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Welles родилась 24 апреля 1569 г. в местности боббингворт, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert Welles было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Hunt, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sylvester Baldwin 28 сентября 1590 г. в местности чолесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 2 декабря 1630 г. в местности освестри, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности освестри, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Welles nació el 24 de abril de 1569, en Bobbingworth, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Welles, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Alice Hunt, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sylvester Baldwin el 28 de septiembre de 1590, en Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 2 de diciembre de 1630, en Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultada en Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
